title: The One with the Secret Closet

jokes.text: You caught me at a bad time. - Are you and Chandler...? - Exactly. - Can I play for you? - No.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if Monica wants to hang out, Monica says it's a bad time
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately assumes they're having sex and Monica confirms with 'Exactly'
jokes.explanation: Quick misdirection comedy - Monica's trying to hide the massage, but Phoebe jumps to the wrong conclusion. Efficient setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Phoebe, I can explain. You see... God, I gotta get better at lying.
jokes.setup: Monica caught in a compromising situation, starts to explain
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up and admits she needs to improve at deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her need for control extends to wanting to be better at lying. Self-aware and economical.

jokes.text: Well, Alexandra has... - It has a name?
jokes.setup: Monica mentions the masseuse's name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treats the massage like Monica's having an affair
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's jealousy escalates the mundane into relationship drama territory. Good escalation beat.

jokes.text: Well, I thought I'd... Yeah, what the hell.
jokes.setup: Chandler caught between Monica and angry Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Decides to abandon Monica and go with Phoebe instead
jokes.explanation: Chandler's cowardly but practical decision to side with the angry person. Classic Chandler self-preservation.

jokes.text: I hope it's not an inappropriate time to say... you're the best sex I've ever had. That's always appropriate.
jokes.setup: Tender moment during childbirth simulation/memory
jokes.punchline: Rachel brings up sex during what should be a pure moment
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate timing humor - sex talk during birth. Ross's matter-of-fact response adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: I hope you're a better father than you are a friend!
jokes.setup: Joey overhears Ross and Rachel's intimate conversation
jokes.punchline: Joey's hurt reaction to being excluded from the moment
jokes.explanation: Joey's childlike hurt feelings expressed as a dramatic insult. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Maybe you should come to me. I'm not wearing any bottoms.
jokes.setup: Joey excited about feeling the baby kick, starts to approach
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals she's not wearing pants, stopping Joey in his tracks
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup with awkward pregnancy logistics. Good visual comedy moment.

jokes.text: Who's that annoying girl soccer player? - Mia Hamm? - Mia Hamm!
jokes.setup: Rachel comparing baby's kicking to a soccer player
jokes.punchline: Can't remember Mia Hamm's name, calls her 'annoying'
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture reference joke. Rachel's pregnancy brain makes her forget names but not attitudes.

jokes.text: One hand on the sheet, Joe.
jokes.setup: Joey feeling Rachel's belly for baby kicks
jokes.punchline: Rachel has to remind Joey about appropriate boundaries
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey boundary issues. Rachel's maternal but firm correction. Good character-driven awkwardness.

jokes.text: Who says you can't get a nice punch bowl for under $6?
jokes.setup: Monica mentions they got a wedding gift from people who don't like them
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic appreciation of the cheap gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm. The specific price point makes it funnier than generic cheapness.

jokes.text: How about to the street, from the balcony?
jokes.setup: Monica asks where they could return the cheap gift
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests throwing it off the balcony
jokes.explanation: Escalates from returning to destroying. Chandler's casual delivery of violent solution is funny.

jokes.text: Let's cross that bridge when they drop by unexpectedly... and demand a gigantic bowl of punch.
jokes.setup: Monica worries about the gift-givers visiting
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagines the absurd scenario of guests demanding punch
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of a mundane situation. The specificity of 'gigantic bowl of punch' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's for canned goods. Have you forgotten everything from orientation?
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests putting the punch bowl in a cabinet
jokes.punchline: Monica acts like their apartment has strict organizational protocols
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica control-freak moment. 'Orientation' suggests she actually gave Chandler apartment training. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Don't try to make me uncomfortable with feminine stuff!
jokes.setup: Monica claims the locked closet contains 'feminine stuff'
jokes.punchline: Chandler calls out her obvious manipulation tactic
jokes.explanation: Chandler sees through Monica's deflection strategy. Good relationship dynamic comedy.

jokes.text: Love you. Hello, Chandler. Lovely day, huh? You.
jokes.setup: Monica sweetly says goodbye to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's ice-cold greeting shows she's still angry
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Monica's warmth and Phoebe's cold anger. The 'You.' at the end is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: If! I said, if it helps.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions Alexandra has been massaging Monica for three years
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to take it back when he realizes it makes things worse
jokes.explanation: Chandler's attempt at damage control after making things worse. Classic nervous backtracking.

jokes.text: That's different. We were roommates. And when?
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she's seen him naked when they lived together
jokes.punchline: Chandler first dismisses it, then immediately gets curious about when
jokes.explanation: Quick turn from dismissal to curiosity. The 'And when?' reveals Chandler's vanity and concern.

jokes.text: I'm curious about the human body.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks when Phoebe saw him naked
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual, scientific-sounding excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe weirdness - treating voyeurism as intellectual curiosity. Delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Or how Ross would feel if he couldn't teach us about dragons. - Dinosaurs. - Potato, potahtoe.
jokes.setup: Phoebe comparing her massage skills to Ross's teaching
jokes.punchline: Gets 'dinosaurs' wrong, then dismisses the correction casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - confidently wrong, then dismissive of facts. 'Potato, potahtoe' is perfect for this situation.

jokes.text: Although we would love to see you do that again.
jokes.setup: Ross does an excited gesture about the baby kicking
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate request to repeat the enthusiastic reaction
jokes.explanation: Joey's lack of filter makes Ross's excitement into entertainment. Mildly uncomfortable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: A dreamless sleep.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions Joey was sleeping when the baby kicked
jokes.punchline: Joey's oddly poetic description of his unconscious state
jokes.explanation: Unexpected eloquence from Joey. The phrase is both funny and oddly beautiful - great character surprise.

jokes.text: I got some stuff going on if you want to feel.
jokes.setup: Ross wishes he could have felt the baby kick
jokes.punchline: Joey offers his own stomach as a substitute
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Joey solution. His helpful nature combined with total inappropriateness creates the comedy.

jokes.text: Can I get a copy of that?
jokes.setup: Ross gives Rachel his pager number for pregnancy emergencies
jokes.punchline: Joey wants to be included in the pregnancy alert system
jokes.explanation: Joey's inclusion anxiety drives him to insert himself into Ross and Rachel's moment. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: That's right, you just enjoy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving Chandler a massage
jokes.punchline: Her encouraging tone sounds sexual in context
jokes.explanation: Sets up the sexual massage noises bit. The phrasing is innocuous but suggestive.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah! - Okay. Oh, God, Phoebe! That's it! That's it, right there!
jokes.setup: Chandler getting a massage from Phoebe
jokes.punchline: His pleasure sounds are completely sexual
jokes.explanation: Peak awkwardness of the massage situation. The escalation from mild to extremely sexual sounds creates good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I'll bet it's Richard. - What? - Well, let me ask you something... when was the last time you saw him?
jokes.setup: Chandler wonders what Monica is hiding in her closet
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests she's literally hiding her ex-boyfriend Richard in there
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Joey logic - why hide things in a closet when you could hide people? The earnest delivery sells it.

jokes.text: You being the 'cake', Richard being the 'too'. - That was off the top of your head? - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Joey explains his 'having cake and eating it too' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Chandler's disbelief that Joey came up with this elaborate theory spontaneously
jokes.explanation: Joey's confident explanation of his complex metaphor followed by Chandler's skepticism. Great character dynamic.

jokes.text: I saw a movie where there was a door. Nobody knew what was behind it. When they finally got it open, millions of bugs came pouring out... and they feasted on human flesh!
jokes.setup: Joey offering another theory about the locked closet
jokes.punchline: Goes from mysterious door to full horror movie scenario
jokes.explanation: Joey's movie-influenced imagination runs wild. Good escalation from mundane to horror movie absurd.

jokes.text: It wouldn't kill you to respect your wife's privacy!
jokes.setup: Joey's horror movie theory about flesh-eating bugs
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic response treating the bug theory as real
jokes.explanation: Chandler's deadpan reaction to Joey's absurdity. Playing it straight against Joey's wild theory works well.

jokes.text: Stupid closet full of bugs!
jokes.setup: Chandler continuing to engage with Joey's bug theory
jokes.punchline: Now genuinely annoyed at the imaginary bugs
jokes.explanation: Chandler's neurosis extends to fictional problems. Good character beat showing how Joey's theories affect him.

jokes.text: Crushing bones! Good thing we're going to a hospital.
jokes.setup: Rachel squeezes Joey's hand during contractions
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual observation about his injury and their convenient location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction - pain but finding the silver lining. Great economy and delivery.

jokes.text: Open it. Open it. Watch your feet there's bugs everywhere.
jokes.setup: Chandler still obsessing over the closet
jokes.punchline: Now completely believes Joey's bug theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the bug theory. Chandler's complete acceptance of the absurd scenario.

jokes.text: Mild discomfort? I take it you've had one of these Braxton thingies?
jokes.setup: Doctor describes contractions as 'mild discomfort'
jokes.punchline: Joey sarcastically asks if the male doctor has personal experience
jokes.explanation: Joey defending Rachel with sarcastic logic. Good protective friend moment with humor.

jokes.text: I wasn't calm! I've never been more scared in my life. - You said everything was okay. - What do I know? I'm not a doctor.
jokes.setup: Rachel thanks Joey for being calm during the scare
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals he was terrified and just pretending to know what he was doing
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation. Joey's protective instincts override his honesty, then he immediately comes clean.

jokes.text: I got half a mind to contract that doctor's uterus. Mild discomfort! What's he talking about?
jokes.setup: Rachel angry about doctor's dismissive description
jokes.punchline: Threatens to 'contract' his uterus (which he doesn't have)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with 'contract' and the impossibility of the threat. Rachel's pregnancy rage is funny.

jokes.text: No uterus, no opinion.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to downplay the contractions since most women don't feel them
jokes.punchline: Rachel shuts him down with simple biological logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and logic. Became a quotable line. Simple, direct, and undeniably correct.

jokes.text: You went to the movies by yourself? - Mona!
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if Ross went to movies alone
jokes.punchline: Ross suddenly remembers his girlfriend and their date
jokes.explanation: Ross's scattered priorities - forgets girlfriend when focused on baby. Good character flaw comedy.

jokes.text: Look, I forgot my underwear.
jokes.setup: Ross needs to go back into the movie theater for something
jokes.punchline: Reveals he forgot underwear, not wanting to admit he was on a date
jokes.explanation: Ross chooses an embarrassing lie over admitting he was with Mona. Awkward but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Hey, not as good as this guy. He brought Twizzlers.
jokes.setup: Nurse says Joey will make a wonderful father
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically points out Joey's candy as superior parenting preparation
jokes.explanation: Ross's jealousy and insecurity about Joey's role. Petty but understandable reaction.

jokes.text: Oh, no, wait. I'm not a 9-year-old girl.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Chandler has a bobby pin
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware correction about his demographics
jokes.explanation: Chandler's self-deprecating awareness of gender stereotypes. Good quick reversal.

jokes.text: Then why do you throw like one?
jokes.setup: Chandler says he's not a 9-year-old girl
jokes.punchline: Joey's quick comeback about his throwing ability
jokes.explanation: Classic schoolyard insult but well-timed. Joey's quick wit catches Chandler off guard.

jokes.text: So how's the inappropriate crush on Rachel coming? Really good. I should be ready to kill myself any day now.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks about Joey's feelings for Rachel
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact description of his emotional progression toward suicide
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and perfectly Joey - treats emotional crisis like a schedule. Great subversion of typical sitcom handling.

jokes.text: Well, you sure found that quick.
jokes.setup: Chandler immediately produces a bobby pin after claiming not to be a 9-year-old girl
jokes.punchline: Joey's dry observation about the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the bobby pin denial. Joey catches Chandler's contradiction with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Except for harboring love for the woman carrying his baby. Well, there is that.
jokes.setup: Chandler says Joey's doing nothing wrong helping Rachel
jokes.punchline: Then immediately points out the major moral complication
jokes.explanation: Chandler's ability to undercut his own reassurance. Good acknowledgment of the actual problem.

jokes.text: Richard, if you're in there, could you pass me my credit card?
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to open the closet with credit card
jokes.punchline: Addresses imaginary Richard as if he's actually in the closet
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Joey's absurd theory. Chandler now playing along with the impossible scenario.

jokes.text: It freaked me out. After a while, I tried to hurt you. It spurred you on.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she raised her massage rates
jokes.punchline: Reveals she tried to cause pain to stop the sex noises, but it made them worse
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation - Phoebe's professional problem-solving backfires. Good uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: What are you looking at? I mean, hi.
jokes.setup: Chandler caught in compromising demonstration of his massage noises
jokes.punchline: Tries to recover with awkward politeness
jokes.explanation: Classic caught-in-the-act moment. Chandler's attempt at normal greeting after being compromised.

jokes.text: It's not because she hates Enya.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Alexandra wears earplugs
jokes.punchline: Reveals the obvious reason while mentioning a completely unrelated possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe randomness - brings up Enya out of nowhere while explaining sex noises. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Well, not clients. Lovers.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she has other clients who make those noises
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects herself to 'lovers'
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's casual correction makes it worse. Good escalation from professional to personal context.

jokes.text: If you stick one hand down your pants you're the spitting image of Joey.
jokes.setup: Ross shows off learning a new trick
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation about Ross resembling Joey's typical posture
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Joey's habits. Chandler's ability to find comedy in mundane moments.

jokes.text: Pickle makes her sick. Giving her pickle is like giving me salad.
jokes.setup: Joey accidentally gives Rachel pickles which make her sick
jokes.punchline: Ross compares it to his own food aversion using analogy
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific analogy. Ross's food issues become a teaching moment. Effective comparison.

jokes.text: Well, if anyone is keeping score, I no longer eat tuna.
jokes.setup: Ross explains pregnancy food aversions
jokes.punchline: Rachel's casual announcement of her own dietary change
jokes.explanation: Rachel's matter-of-fact delivery of information everyone should know. Good understated comedy.

jokes.text: Are you breaking up with us?
jokes.setup: Joey wants to have a serious talk about their living situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel treats it like a romantic relationship ending
jokes.explanation: Treating roommate situation like romantic relationship. Good absurdist take on living arrangements.

jokes.text: I'm dating Mona. Damn it, Mona!
jokes.setup: Ross explaining the arrangement wouldn't be romantic
jokes.punchline: Suddenly remembers he's forgotten his girlfriend again
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ross's scattered priorities. His guilt about forgetting Mona becomes a running gag.

jokes.text: Living with the father? It's a bit conventional for us.
jokes.setup: Considering moving in with Ross to raise the baby
jokes.punchline: Rachel notes this is unusually normal for their group
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the group's unusual relationship dynamics. Self-aware humor about their unconventional lives.

jokes.text: Well, I was due.
jokes.setup: Everyone praising Joey's smart idea about the living arrangement
jokes.punchline: Joey's modest acknowledgment that he was overdue for good thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey self-awareness about his intellectual track record. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You're messy. - No! You weren't supposed to see this! - I married Fred Sanford.
jokes.setup: Chandler discovers Monica's secret messy closet
jokes.punchline: Monica's horror at being discovered, Chandler's Sanford and Son reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character reveal - her biggest shame is being messy. The Fred Sanford reference is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Okay, okay, fine! Now you know. I'm sick.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to explain her organizational system
jokes.punchline: Gives up and admits she considers herself sick for having mess
jokes.explanation: Monica's dramatic self-diagnosis for having one messy space. Perfect character neurosis.

jokes.text: I don't love you because you're organized. I love you in spite of that.
jokes.setup: Monica worrying about Chandler's reaction to her mess
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals her organization is actually the annoying trait
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal of expectations. Sweet sentiment delivered with comedy. Great relationship humor.

jokes.text: I have thrown back a lot of orange juice with calcium. It's a couple weeks past its expiration date, so it's got a bit of a kick.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining how he's dealing with Rachel moving out
jokes.punchline: Treats expired orange juice like alcohol, describing it having a 'kick'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Joey logic - treating spoiled juice like hard liquor. Perfect character-specific coping mechanism.

jokes.text: I'm a lone wolf. A loner. Alone. All alone. Forever. What's a wolf gotta do to get a hug around here?
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he's better off alone now that Rachel moved out
jokes.punchline: Escalates to dramatic loneliness then immediately asks for comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey emotional journey - from bravado to vulnerability in seconds. The wolf metaphor breaking down is perfect.

jokes.text: Phoebe? - Yeah, say my name. Say it.
jokes.setup: Chandler getting another massage from Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe encourages his sexual-sounding responses
jokes.explanation: Peak awkwardness of the massage storyline. Phoebe now encouraging the behavior she previously found disturbing.

tmdb_overview: Chandler becomes curious about a locked bathroom closet. Monica makes unusual sounds during Phoebe's massage.
